title: T-093 — API/backfill: NPD Builder writes initial shared BOM version and switches readers to BOM SSOT [PATCH: release orchestrator handoff]
prompt:
# T-093 — NPD Builder writes initial shared BOM version into shared BOM SSOT

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md 2026-05-03 FINAL Amendment + _meta/decisions/2026-05-03-flow-d365-settings-technical-decisions.md
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Final decisions this task must implement
- NPD Builder is the Monopilot-owned release action that creates WIP/intermediate items, the FG and the initial shared BOM/product-spec version after required departments close and approval passes.
- Shared `bom_headers`/`bom_lines` are SSOT across NPD, Technical, Planning, Production and integrations.
- D365 is optional/export/import integration only; it must not be source of truth and must not drive canonical NPD Builder state.
- Released product/BOM/spec edits from NPD must create a new version and route to Technical approval.

## Goal
Switch NPD Builder and NPD BOM readers from legacy computed BOM behavior to the shared BOM SSOT introduced/verified by T-092. This task is ACTIONABLE after T-092; it is blocked only if T-092 has not landed.

## Implementation contract
1. Discover current NPD Builder/release actions, FA/FG creation actions, BOM readers and legacy computed view names before editing.
2. Add/replace a server action/domain service such as `createInitialSharedBomVersionForNpdProject(projectId)` or equivalent naming used by the codebase. It must run inside the NPD Builder release transaction that creates/maps WIP/intermediate items and FG.
3. Inputs: NPD project (`DEV-123`), compatibility FA/product code if still present, prod_detail/components, packaging/MRP components, quantities/UOMs, route/process references and approval context.
4. Outputs:
   - exactly one initial `bom_headers` row per released FG/project/version (idempotent on retry);
   - corresponding `bom_lines` rows from the same component set previously shown by the computed BOM preview;
   - version metadata (`version='1.0.0'` or project-configured initial version), `origin_module='npd'`, status not factory-active until configured release/Technical rules are satisfied;
   - outbox event such as `bom.initial_version_created` and release event linking project → FG → BOM version.
5. Update NPD BOM readers/previews to read from shared `bom_headers/bom_lines` by project/FG/product id. The UI may still display a computed preview before release, but canonical post-release reads must use shared BOM rows.
6. Add an explicit post-release edit path guard: any NPD edit to released product/BOM/factory-spec data must call/create a new version and submit it to Technical approval; direct in-place update of active approved version must fail. If the full edit UI is elsewhere, add the domain guard/tests here and document downstream call contract.
7. Backfill migration `0093_shared_bom_npd_backfill.sql` (or next available sequence): for existing released/built FGs without shared BOM headers, create initial shared BOM versions from legacy computed data. Do not import D365 as authority.
8. After backfill and reader switch are verified, drop or fully de-canonicalize the legacy NPD computed BOM view. If dropping is unsafe due to compatibility, leave a compatibility view that reads from shared BOM and is labelled non-canonical.

## Acceptance criteria
- Given NPD Builder releases a project with 3 components, when the transaction commits, then WIP/intermediate items + FG + exactly one initial shared BOM version and 3+ shared bom_lines exist.
- Given the release action is retried after a transient error, when rerun for the same project/version, then no duplicate BOM header or lines are created.
- Given NPD BOM reader is called for a released FG, when traced/grepped, then it reads shared BOM SSOT and not the old computed view as canonical.
- Given a released FG active BOM exists, when NPD requests a BOM/product-spec edit, then a new version is created and marked pending Technical approval; the active approved version is not mutated.
- Given Technical rejects/pends a new version, when factory/Planning reads active BOM, then the prior approved active version remains in use.
- Given D365 import/cache/export data differs from Monopilot shared BOM, when NPD Builder/release/readers run, then Monopilot shared BOM remains canonical and D365 data is not silently applied.
- Given backfill runs against existing released/built FGs, when complete, then every released/built FG has a shared BOM header and no duplicates.

## Test strategy
- RED unit tests for idempotent initial BOM creation and N-components → N-lines.
- RED integration test for NPD Builder release transaction creating WIP + FG + BOM version atomically.
- RED reader test/grep guard proving released BOM reads do not depend on old computed view.
- RED post-release edit test proving new version + Technical approval pending state and no in-place active mutation.
- Migration dry-run fixture with released/built FGs and D365 mismatch fixture.

## Risk red lines
- Do not run before T-092 schema/lifecycle lands.
- Do not create a separate NPD-only BOM authority.
- Do not auto-promote a post-release edited version to active without Technical approval.
- Do not use D365 import/cache as canonical source or silently overwrite Monopilot shared BOM from D365.
- Do not duplicate BOM lines on retries/backfill.
- Do not mix this with broad FA→FG route/DB/event rename.


## 2026-05-03 E2E spine patch — scope boundary with release orchestrator
T-093 remains the shared BOM SSOT write/backfill task, but it must be called by the canonical NPD Builder release orchestrator in T-096. It must not independently decide that an FG is factory-usable. Factory usability requires Technical approval of the initial factory_spec/BOM bundle and release status transition from T-097.

Additional constraints:
- Use FG canonical terminology; FA only as compatibility alias.
- NPD Builder writes initial shared BOM from Monopilot-owned data, not D365.
- The active factory-consumable BOM/spec pair must require Technical approval before factory/Planning use.
- D365 optional export may consume released data but must never set release state.

pipeline_inputs.description: ACTIONABLE after T-092: NPD Builder writes initial shared BOM version with WIP/FG release, switches readers/backfill to shared BOM SSOT, and enforces new version + Technical approval for post-release edits.
pipeline_inputs.details: NPD Builder writes initial shared BOM and readers/backfill switch to BOM SSOT with idempotency checklist.
scope_files: Existing NPD Builder/release actions/readers/migrations/tests; exact paths must be discovered before editing
Legacy computed BOM view migration or compatibility view after backfill
out_of_scope: Technical approval UI beyond required pending-status/domain guard
acceptance_criteria: Release with components creates exactly one initial shared BOM header/version and lines and returns IDs/status to release orchestrator.
Readers/backfill use shared BOM SSOT and existing FGs receive one backfilled version without duplicates.
Post-release edit creates pending Technical approval version while prior active approved version remains factory-readable.
D365 export disabled/present does not affect BOM creation or mark factory use active.
test_strategy: RED unit tests for idempotency and N-components → N-lines.
RED integration test for getFaBom returning header-driven rows.
RED migration dry-run test for 5-FA fixture.
Grep guard for legacy view name.
risk_red_lines: Do not run before T-092 lands.
Do not create a separate NPD-only BOM authority.
Do not auto-promote post-release edits to active without Technical approval.
Do not treat D365 as source of truth or silently overwrite Monopilot shared BOM from D365.
Do not duplicate BOM lines on retries/backfill.
Do not mix with broad FA→FG route/DB/event rename.
Do not duplicate release orchestration logic here; T-096 owns transaction ordering.
Do not mark shared BOM active for factory use before Technical approval.
